message:
fix(fr-runtime): gate DEBUG on enable-debug-command (Redis 7.2 default-deny) [bd-frankenredis-j29y]

Upstream Redis 7.2 defaults `enable-debug-command no` and rejects
DEBUG with the canonical lockout error
"ERR DEBUG command not allowed. If the enable-debug-command
option is set to local, you can run it from a local connection,
otherwise you need to set this option in the configuration file,
and then restart the server."
This is a security gate — operators must opt in via the
config-file directive or `--enable-debug-command yes` at startup.

fr-runtime previously exposed the knob as a static CONFIG GET
value defaulted to "no" but never enforced the gate: DEBUG was
dispatched unconditionally to fr-command::debug_cmd. An end-to-end
smoke comparing 30 commands against vendored redis-server 7.2.4
caught it (every other command matched; only DEBUG diverged: fr
returned a normal subcommand error while real redis returned the
lockout).

Plumbing:
  • ServerState gains `enable_debug_command: String` (default "no").
  • Runtime::set_enable_debug_command(&str) accepts no/local/yes
    case-insensitive; unknown tokens normalize to "no" (safe).
  • RuntimeSpecialCommand::Debug intercepts before fr-command's
    dispatch. handle_debug_command_gate returns Some(error_frame)
    when the gate denies and None when it allows (so the existing
    fall-through to dispatch_argv + debug_cmd handles the
    subcommand). "local" treats like "yes" — fr-server binds to
    127.0.0.1 by default, making this safe in typical deployments;
    a future per-session peer check can tighten without changing
    the wire shape.
  • CONFIG GET emits the dynamic runtime value; the static
    CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS loop now skips "enable-debug-command" to
    avoid double-emit. CONFIG SET still rejects as immutable.
  • fr-server: --enable-debug-command CLI flag + the
    enable-debug-command directive in --config startup file. CLI
    wins. Help text updated.

Tests:
  • 11 fr-runtime tests using DEBUG explicitly opt-in via
    set_enable_debug_command("yes").
  • New debug_default_denies_with_upstream_lockout_error pins the
    canonical wording.
  • Conformance fixtures core_debug.json, core_connection.json,
    core_server.json get the opt-in via configure_runtime_for_fixture
    (mirrors VendoredRedis::spawn_with_flags passing
    --enable-debug-command yes).

E2E verified end-to-end:
  Default fr-server: DEBUG OBJECT k → "ERR DEBUG command not allowed..."
  --enable-debug-command yes: DEBUG OBJECT k → normal embstr reply.
  CONFIG GET enable-debug-command reflects the live value.

Test sweep: fr-store 310, fr-command 668, fr-runtime 287 lib + 43
admin_commands_test, fr-conformance 30 conformance_core, fr-protocol
58 — all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -35,7 +35,7 @@
 {"id": "frankenredis-1qdg", "title": "Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands", "description": "# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-1rzz", "title": "Repair stale Beads SQLite database from JSONL", "description": "br sync --flush-only refuses to export because .beads/beads.db has fewer issues than issues.jsonl. Snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated DB from JSONL, verify doctor/status/show, then promote without deleting old DB artifacts.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-25T01:47:41.473719041Z", "created_by": "codex-beads-repair", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.643357040Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.640812365Z", "close_reason": "DB recovery shipped via hs8w sister bead. Procedure documented + executed: (1) cp .beads/beads.db backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with UNIQUE constraint due to duplicate-row corruption, mv the canonical .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild (imports JSONL→fresh DB cleanly). Current state verified: 'br doctor' reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (806/806), sync.metadata in sync, schema.tables OK, schema.columns OK, write_probe OK. Snapshots preserved at .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_1777347889 and .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_1777347912 (gitignored). The 'snapshot, rebuild isolated, verify, promote' acceptance criterion has been met end-to-end.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-1uin", "title": "[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save", "description": "Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z"}
+{"id":"frankenredis-1uin","title":"[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save","description":"Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "title": "Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake", "description": "# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "title": "Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline", "description": "# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:23.224067917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922840524Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922656586Z", "close_reason": "Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-gess", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.702954088Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
@@ -438,6 +438,7 @@
 {"id": "frankenredis-irk8", "title": "EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in", "description": "# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-06pn", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-42df", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-6axy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-e1e9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-v7zc", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-ix1", "title": "Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-03-20T20:27:04.284868059Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-20T20:30:12.689735969Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-20T20:30:12.687284050Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-iz1j", "title": "Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync", "description": "# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z", "close_reason": "Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-iz1j", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-j29y","title":"fr-runtime: gate DEBUG command on enable-debug-command config (matches upstream Redis 7.2 default-deny)","description":"fr-runtime exposes enable-debug-command as a static CONFIG GET value defaulted to 'no', matching upstream's CLI/config-file knob. But the DEBUG command is dispatched unconditionally in fr-command — no gate is actually enforced. Upstream Redis 7.2 rejects DEBUG with the canonical error 'ERR DEBUG command not allowed. If the enable-debug-command option is set to local, you can run it from a local connection, otherwise you need to set this option in the configuration file, and then restart the server.' when enable-debug-command=no (the default) and the connection isn't a special debug peer. fr accepts arbitrary DEBUG subcommands instead, surfaced by an end-to-end smoke comparing 30 commands against vendored redis 7.2.4. Acceptance: in default config, DEBUG OBJECT returns the upstream-shape lockout error; with enable-debug-command=yes (set via config file at startup, not CONFIG SET — that one is immutable), DEBUG runs as today. Gate must be in fr-runtime so command-table tests still pass; tests should set the CLI flag.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T21:31:35.781020356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:53:26.920561057Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:53:26.917639123Z","close_reason":"fr-runtime now intercepts DEBUG via RuntimeSpecialCommand::Debug + handle_debug_command_gate, returning the canonical upstream Redis 7.2 lockout error when enable_debug_command is 'no' (default). New Runtime::set_enable_debug_command setter; --enable-debug-command CLI + config-file directive plumbing; CONFIG GET emits the live value. 11 fr-runtime tests opt-in to DEBUG via the setter; new debug_default_denies_with_upstream_lockout_error pins the default-deny wording. Conformance fixtures opt in via configure_runtime_for_fixture. E2E verified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-j31m", "title": "Replication ack monotonicity should allow negative ACKs and preserve prior offset", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-13T19:02:16.480579621Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-13T19:15:03.260813195Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-13T19:15:03.260637452Z", "close_reason": "Behavior already correct: negative ACKs are rejected with error per conformance test replconf_ack_negative_rejected. Monotonicity is also enforced - lower offsets don't update state.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-j4vq", "title": "CRITICAL: FUNCTION STATS test expects Integer(0) but pinned impl + upstream return nil (BulkString(None))", "description": "## Summary

`fr-command::tests::function_list_and_stats_match_redis_reply_shapes` fails on fresh HEAD (eb6b915). The test asserts that `FUNCTION STATS` emits `Integer(0)` in the `running_script` slot, but the implementation at `fr-command/src/lib.rs:6394` intentionally emits `RespFrame::BulkString(None)` with a `PINNED 2026-04-22` comment citing live redis:7.4.8 evidence.

A fresh probe against vendored `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server` (7.2.4) confirms the implementation is correct:

```
redis-cli --no-raw FUNCTION STATS
1) \"running_script\"
2) (nil)
3) \"engines\"
...
```

So the implementation matches upstream; the **test assertion** is the bug.

## How it got this way

`git show fd17b663` (titled "docs(command): pin FUNCTION LIST/STATS reply shape to live redis:7.4 behavior") is self-contradictory:

- Adds a long `PINNED 2026-04-22` comment at `lib.rs:6388-6393` asserting `running_script` must be `BulkString(None)` per live 7.4.8 evidence.
- In the same commit, flips the test assertion from `BulkString(None)` to `Integer(0)`:
  ```
  -                RespFrame::BulkString(None),
  +                RespFrame::Integer(0),
  ```
- Commit message says "No behavioural change — the three lines at 6317 / 6377 / 6380 already carried the correct values in the current tree. This commit only adds the pinning comments so the oscillation stops." — but the test diff contradicts the commit message.

The test diff at line 30431 was the mistake; the pin to `BulkString(None)` in the source is the canonical answer.

## Failure

```
thread 'tests::function_list_and_stats_match_redis_reply_shapes' panicked at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:30427:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: ... BulkString(None) ...   // actual from dispatch
  right: ... Integer(0) ...        // test expectation
```

## Suggested fix direction

Single-line revert of the test assertion:

```diff
-                RespFrame::Integer(0),
```
